review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Organisation Culture:

Typical "Sarkari". Bosses only believe in time pass and unnecessary gyaan instead of talking business. Management goes haywire when competition surpasses them but have no concrete strategy to regain the position and increase market share.


Poor customer service (Biggest Blunder):

In a real competitive world, "customer is the king" but somehow senior management at SBI card has this hollow arrogance and swag of being subsidiary of SBI and hence treats their clients very poorly. They don't offer what client needs and what competition us already offering to the client, they wants to shove it down to their throat what SBI Card wants to offer. And has this autocratic mentality of "we don't care what client wants, he has to take it what we give, we are not here to comfort the client, client is here to give us business." Going to office everyday has been a headache, as everyday you see a long queue of disappointed, dissatisfied, frustrated and angry customers waiting at the security entrance and shouting at the top of their voice, fighting and sometimes even getting violent with SBI Card employees because of the kind of service given to them and false commitments not being fulfilled after they became SBI card's unfortunate customers. 


Fear and everyday Humiliation:

Instead of motivation and encouragement, bosses will always make you feel inferior and what a disappoint you are to the entire human race. Instead of making the employee feel comfortable and satisfied at the work place, employees on the floor are always tensed, frustrated, sad faced and depressed. Employees are afraid and scared to speak their mind and becomes a "yes-man" employee to their bosses.


Lack of tech savviness:

Analogically, company is still riding on a Fiat in an F1 race.


High employee attrition:

As SBI card does not consider employees as a resource, even employees do not feel the sense of loyalty towards SBI card and joins some other organisation where they atleast don't go to office with a sense of fear, Humiliation and depression. Surprisingly, high attrition is observed at middle and bottom level management, but in SBI Card its a different story, here CEO gets changed every 3 years. And hence, there is no stability in company policy as every new CEO will try to run the company as per his wish (after all its a "sarkari company").

I would never ever recommend anybody to join this organisation.
